job_statement: When setting up Argo CD Agent across hub and spoke clusters, I want to understand the required namespaces, contexts, and CLI parameters, so I can correctly configure the multi-cluster architecture.
related_jobs: Install Principal component; Set up spoke cluster environment
desired_outcomes: Minimize configuration errors from incorrect parameter usage; Ensure correct namespace mapping between hub and spoke clusters; Reduce time to understand multi-cluster setup requirements
evidence: Lines 17-42: Argo CD Agent Terminologies section defines principal namespace, agent namespace, context, principal context, and agent context with examples
notes: Conceptual foundation for understanding the Principal-Agent architecture. Critical for avoiding namespace and context confusion during setup.

job_statement: When establishing the control plane for multi-cluster GitOps, I want to install and configure the Principal component with secure PKI, so I can centrally manage Agent communications across workload clusters.
prerequisites: Install GitOps Operator on hub cluster; Install cluster-scoped Argo CD instance; Configure Apps in any namespace feature
related_jobs: Set up spoke cluster environment; Create Agent secrets
desired_outcomes: Minimize time to establish secure hub-spoke communication; Ensure proper PKI initialization for production security; Reduce likelihood of misconfigured namespace mappings
evidence: Lines 44-120: Full procedure for deploying Principal component, creating namespaces, configuring AppProject, and initializing PKI with CA, server certificates, and JWT keys
notes: Multi-step consumption chain job. Includes WARNING that CLI-generated PKI is for dev/test only - production requires proper CA. Critical that sourceNamespaces match Agent names exactly.

job_statement: As a Platform administrator using the Argo CD CR, when deploying the Principal component, I want to enable it with proper auth and TLS configuration, so I can secure all Principal-Agent communications.
parent_job: Install and configure the Principal component with secure PKI
prerequisites: Have GitOps Operator installed; Have cluster-scoped Argo CD instance capability
desired_outcomes: Ensure all communications are encrypted with mTLS; Prevent use of insecure auto-generated certificates in production; Minimize setup errors from incorrect auth configuration
evidence: Lines 50-64: Argo CD CR configuration showing argoCDAgent.principal section with auth, logLevel, namespace, tls, and jwt settings
notes: CR-based approach for enabling Principal. Note that sourceNamespaces must match Agent names or deployment/monitoring fails.

section: Setting up a spoke cluster environment
job_statement: When connecting Agent clusters to the Principal, I want to create Agent secrets and propagate CA certificates, so I can establish secure authenticated connections between hub and spoke.
prerequisites: Complete Principal setup; Have argocd-agentctl CLI installed; Have helm CLI installed (>v3.8.0); Have access to both Principal and Agent clusters
related_jobs: Install Principal component; Install Agent component
desired_outcomes: Minimize manual certificate management overhead; Ensure proper CA propagation across clusters; Reduce time to establish trust between Principal and Agent
evidence: Lines 122-145: Procedure for creating Agent secret on Principal cluster, copying CA certificate to Agent cluster, generating client certificate for Agent, and verifying secrets
notes: Critical setup step that enables Principal-Agent secure communication. Uses argocd-agentctl for PKI management across clusters.

section: Installing the Argo CD Agent by using a Helm chart
job_statement: When deploying Agent components on workload clusters, I want to choose between managed and autonomous modes, so I can align Agent behavior with my organizational control model.
prerequisites: Understand Argo CD Agent architecture; Have Principal component running; Have spoke cluster environment set up
related_jobs: Deploy Argo CD instance on Agent cluster; Install Agent in managed mode; Install Agent in autonomous mode
desired_outcomes: Minimize time to determine correct deployment mode for each workload cluster; Ensure alignment between control model and technical implementation; Reduce likelihood of deploying wrong mode for organizational needs
evidence: Lines 147-155: Overview section explaining Helm-based installation process and the two modes: managed (Principal controls lifecycle) vs autonomous (Agent independent)
notes: Conceptual decision point. Managed mode = hub is source of truth; Autonomous mode = spoke is source of truth. This choice affects where applications are created and managed.

section: Deploying an Argo CD instance on the Agent cluster
job_statement: When preparing to install the Agent component, I want to deploy a minimal Argo CD instance on the workload cluster, so I can provide the application controller needed for Agent operations.
parent_job: Choose between managed and autonomous modes
prerequisites: Have GitOps Operator installed on Agent cluster; Have Agent namespace created
related_jobs: Install Agent in managed mode; Install Agent in autonomous mode
desired_outcomes: Minimize resource overhead by disabling unnecessary components; Ensure Argo CD is ready before Agent installation; Reduce deployment failures from missing dependencies
evidence: Lines 157-170: Procedure showing Argo CD CR with server disabled, creating minimal instance for Agent workload handling
notes: Prerequisite step for Helm-based Agent installation. CR disables server component since Agent provides its own interface to Principal.

section: Installing the Argo CD Agent in managed mode by using a Helm chart
job_statement: As a Platform administrator using Helm, when deploying managed-mode Agents, I want to configure network policies and Helm values, so I can enable Principal-controlled application deployment to workload clusters.
parent_job: Choose between managed and autonomous modes
prerequisites: Deploy Argo CD instance on Agent cluster; Have Principal component running; Have spoke cluster environment configured
related_jobs: Install Agent in autonomous mode; Deploy applications in managed mode
desired_outcomes: Minimize connectivity issues by properly configuring network policies; Ensure Agent can communicate with Redis for state synchronization; Reduce time to enable hub-driven application deployments
evidence: Lines 172-186: Procedure for creating custom NetworkPolicy to allow Agent-Redis communication, adding Helm repo, and installing with managed mode parameters including Redis configuration
notes: Managed mode requires custom NetworkPolicy because default GitOps Operator policy blocks Agent-Redis communication. Hub is source of truth in this mode.

section: Installing the Argo CD Agent in autonomous mode by using a Helm chart
job_statement: As a Platform administrator using Helm, when deploying autonomous-mode Agents, I want to configure network policies, Helm values, and AppProject resources, so I can enable spoke-controlled application deployment with hub visibility.
parent_job: Choose between managed and autonomous modes
prerequisites: Deploy Argo CD instance on Agent cluster; Have Principal component running; Have spoke cluster environment configured
related_jobs: Install Agent in managed mode; Deploy applications in autonomous mode
desired_outcomes: Enable spoke teams to manage their own applications while maintaining hub visibility; Ensure proper AppProject configuration for autonomous operation; Minimize manual configuration overhead for spoke cluster teams
evidence: Lines 188-202: Procedure for creating NetworkPolicy, installing autonomous Agent via Helm, and creating AppProject with wildcard permissions in agent namespace
notes: Autonomous mode requires AppProject creation in agent namespace. Spoke is source of truth - hub only observes. NetworkPolicy requirement same as managed mode.

section: Installing the Argo CD Agent by using an Argo CD custom resource
job_statement: When I prefer operator-managed Agent deployment over Helm, I want to configure the Agent component via Argo CD CR, so I can leverage GitOps Operator lifecycle management for Agent components.
parent_job: Choose between managed and autonomous modes
prerequisites: Have required secrets created for Agent component; Have cluster-scoped Argo CD instance capability
related_jobs: Install Agent via Helm in managed mode; Install Agent via Helm in autonomous mode
desired_outcomes: Minimize operational overhead by using operator-managed lifecycle; Ensure consistent configuration across Agent deployments; Reduce manual Helm chart version management
evidence: Lines 204-226: Procedure showing Argo CD CR configurations for both managed and autonomous modes with argoCDAgent.agent section including client, redis, and tls settings
notes: Alternative to Helm-based installation. CR-based approach integrates with GitOps Operator for lifecycle management. Same mode selection (managed vs autonomous) applies.

section: Verifying the Argo CD Agent installation in Managed Mode or Autonomous Mode
job_statement: When completing Agent installation, I want to verify pod health, connection status, and Principal-Agent communication, so I can confirm the setup is working correctly before deploying applications.
prerequisites: Complete Agent installation (Helm or CR-based); Have Principal component running
desired_outcomes: Minimize time to identify misconfigured Agents before application deployment; Ensure bidirectional Principal-Agent communication is established; Reduce application deployment failures from unverified Agent state
evidence: Lines 228-253: Verification procedure checking Agent pod status, connection logs showing 'Authentication successful' and 'Connected' messages, optional metrics verification, and Principal-side connection confirmation
notes: Critical verification step. Successful verification shows 'Authentication successful' and 'Connected' in Agent logs, and 'agent connected' in Principal logs. Includes optional metrics check and agent list command.

job_statement: When I need to deploy applications using the Agent architecture, I want to understand which cluster is the source of truth for my chosen mode, so I can create applications in the correct location.
prerequisites: Complete Agent installation and verification; Understand managed vs autonomous mode implications
related_jobs: Deploy application in managed mode; Deploy application in autonomous mode
desired_outcomes: Minimize application deployment errors from creating resources in wrong cluster; Ensure alignment between mode choice and application creation location; Reduce confusion about source of truth for each Agent mode
evidence: Lines 255-260: Conceptual overview explaining that managed mode uses hub as source of truth, autonomous mode uses spoke as source of truth
notes: Decision point for application deployment. Mode determines where Application CR is created and which cluster controls the configuration.

section: Deploying an Argo CD application in managed mode
job_statement: As a Platform administrator in managed mode, when deploying applications, I want to create Application resources in the hub cluster, so I can centrally control application deployment to spoke clusters.
parent_job: Deploy applications using the Agent architecture
prerequisites: Have Principal and Agent deployed and verified; Have oc CLI with hub and spoke contexts configured
desired_outcomes: Minimize deployment errors by creating applications in correct cluster; Ensure spoke cluster changes are automatically reverted by hub; Enable central control of application lifecycle across spokes
evidence: Lines 262-278: Procedure for creating Application manifest with agent-managed namespace and destination.name pointing to agent, applying to hub cluster, and verifying creation on spoke and sync status on hub
notes: Hub is source of truth - applications created on hub, Agent creates them on spoke. Direct changes on spoke are reverted. Uses destination.name instead of server URL.

section: Deploying an Argo CD application in autonomous mode
job_statement: As a Platform administrator in autonomous mode, when deploying applications, I want to create Application resources in the spoke cluster, so I can enable spoke teams to control their deployments while maintaining hub visibility.
parent_job: Deploy applications using the Agent architecture
prerequisites: Have Principal and Agent deployed and verified; Have oc CLI with hub and spoke contexts configured
desired_outcomes: Enable spoke team autonomy for application management; Ensure hub maintains visibility into spoke application state; Prevent unauthorized hub-side modifications to spoke applications
evidence: Lines 280-294: Procedure for creating Application manifest with destination.server as kubernetes.default.svc (local cluster), applying to spoke cluster, verifying creation on hub and sync status on spoke
notes: Spoke is source of truth - applications created on spoke, Agent reports state to hub. Hub changes not allowed. Uses destination.server for local deployment.

section: Troubleshooting Principal-Agent communication and deployment issues
job_statement: When encountering Principal-Agent connectivity or deployment problems, I want diagnostic steps for common failure scenarios, so I can quickly identify and resolve issues.
related_jobs: Verify Agent installation; Install Principal component; Set up spoke cluster environment
desired_outcomes: Minimize downtime from Agent connectivity failures; Reduce time to identify root cause of communication issues; Ensure proper resolution of TLS handshake and network policy problems
evidence: Lines 296-332: Troubleshooting reference covering: Agent cannot connect to Principal (pod health, hostname, certificates), Agent cannot connect to Redis (pod health, address, secrets, NetworkPolicy), Agent handshake fails (TLS certificate validation issues), Agent cannot view resources (RBAC permissions)
notes: Reference material for common failure modes. Covers connectivity, authentication, and authorization issues. Includes specific error messages to watch for in logs.
